title: Gleichenia sartorii

Gleicheniaceae is a family of ferns, and Gleichenia sartorii is specifically classified as a type of fern. The family Gleicheniaceae is part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. intro: Gleichenia sartorii, also known as Sartor's tree fern or Sartor's spleenwort, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gleicheniaceae, characterized by its tree-like habit and fern-like fronds. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects, which may help to protect against oxidative stress and support immune function. Bioactive constituents of Gleichenia sartorii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Gleichenia sartorii is typically used to make tea, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
